title: A systematic review of the neurophysiology of mindfulness on EEG oscillations
headline: A systematic review of the neurophysiology of mindfulness on EEG oscillations
summary: A meta-analysis / systematic review of mindfulness / meditation examined brain & mechanisms. The systematic review revealed that mindfulness was most commonly associated with enhanced alpha and theta power as compared to an eyes closed resting state, although such outcomes were not uniformly reported. In summary, mindfulness is associated with increased alpha and theta power in both healthy individuals and in patient groups.
keyFindings: The systematic review revealed that mindfulness was most commonly associated with enhanced alpha and theta power as compared to an eyes closed resting state, although such outcomes were not uniformly reported.
In summary, mindfulness is associated with increased alpha and theta power in both healthy individuals and in patient groups.
limitations: Pooled estimates combine heterogeneous studies; effect sizes vary by practice, population, and control condition.
The practice studied (mindfulness / meditation) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
simplifiedTitle: A systematic review of the neurophysiology of mindfulness on EEG oscillations
aiSnippet: Meta-analysis / Systematic review, Neuroscience and biobehavioral reviews (2015). The systematic review revealed that mindfulness was most commonly associated with enhanced alpha and theta power as compared to an eyes closed resting state, although such outcomes were not uniformly reported. In summary, mindfulness is associated with increased alpha and theta power in both healthy individuals and in patient groups.
whyItMatters: MoM shares directed attention (the focused-attention component) with the practice studied here. Because the benefit is linked to that mechanism rather than to any one method, it's reasonable to expect it may extend to MoM as well — though MoM hasn't been studied directly for this outcome.
